skill.name: skill-creator
skill.description: Guide for creating and updating AI skills with clear structure, reusable resources, validation steps, and packaging support.
skill.summary: Create effective AI skills with templates, workflows, validation, and packaging guidance.
skill.author: 7Spade

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and critical patterns, but review found those are mainly false positives from Markdown fences, Apache license URLs, and template text. No evidence found of prompt injection, network exfiltration, credential files, weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, or command execution. The remaining publishable risk is filesystem behavior: scripts can create files and recursively package folder contents.

medium_findings.title: Unvalidated Skill Name Used in Filesystem Path
medium_findings.description: The initializer combines a user-provided path and skill name to create directories and files, but it does not validate the skill name before using it in the path. A careless or untrusted invocation could create files outside the intended naming policy.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly derives the output directory from user arguments and writes files there. The risk depends on who supplies arguments, so this is a moderate filesystem concern rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Recursive Packaging Can Include Unintended Files
medium_findings.description: The packaging script walks every file under the selected skill directory and writes it into the .skill archive. If secrets, private notes, or unrelated files are present in that folder, they can be distributed accidentally.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The recursive archive behavior is explicit in the code. There is no evidence of exfiltration, but accidental inclusion is a realistic publishing risk.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings occur in Markdown fences, inline file names, examples, and template text. No evidence found of subprocess calls, shell execution, eval, or similar executable command dispatch.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation examples or Python string templates, not executed shell syntax. A targeted search found no command execution APIs in the scanned files.

low_findings.title: Network and Weak Cryptography Findings Are Static False Positives
low_findings.description: The only hardcoded URLs are Apache License references, and no evidence found of network requests or cryptographic operations. Weak cryptography and heuristic combination alerts appear caused by ordinary documentation text and filenames.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited URLs are license text and the cited code validates YAML keys. No network client, credential access, certificate material, or crypto primitive was found in the reviewed files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unvalidated Skill Name Used in Filesystem Path
dangerous_patterns.description: The initializer combines a user-provided path and skill name to create directories and files, but it does not validate the skill name before using it in the path. A careless or untrusted invocation could create files outside the intended naming policy.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code directly derives the output directory from user arguments and writes files there. The risk depends on who supplies arguments, so this is a moderate filesystem concern rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Recursive Packaging Can Include Unintended Files
dangerous_patterns.description: The packaging script walks every file under the selected skill directory and writes it into the .skill archive. If secrets, private notes, or unrelated files are present in that folder, they can be distributed accidentally.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The recursive archive behavior is explicit in the code. There is no evidence of exfiltration, but accidental inclusion is a realistic publishing risk.

content.value_statement: Teams often struggle to turn repeatable AI workflows into reliable skill packages. This skill provides structured guidance, templates, validation checks, and packaging steps for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code users.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to structure SKILL.md with useful frontmatter and concise instructions.
Guides selection of scripts, references, and assets for reusable skill packages.
Provides workflow and output pattern references for skill authors.
Includes an initializer script that creates a template skill directory.
Includes validation and packaging scripts for distributable .skill files.
Advises authors to remove unneeded generated files before packaging.
limitations: Does not create domain expertise without user-provided examples or requirements.
Generated template files still require manual review and cleanup.
Packaging can include unintended files if the skill folder is not checked first.
Validation is basic and does not replace a full security review.

use_cases.title: Create a New Team Skill
use_cases.description: Turn a repeated team workflow into a structured skill with instructions, scripts, references, and assets.

use_cases.title: Improve an Existing Skill
use_cases.description: Review a current skill for clearer triggers, better resource organization, and more reliable workflow guidance.

use_cases.title: Package Skills for Distribution
use_cases.description: Validate a skill folder and package it into a distributable file after final cleanup.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new skill scaffold.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a new skill for [workflow]. Ask only the key questions needed, then plan the SKILL.md structure and required resources.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Reusable Resources
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request to decide what belongs in a skill package.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these example user requests for a skill: [examples]. Recommend which instructions, scripts, references, and assets should be included.

prompt_templates.title: Refine an Existing Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request to improve clarity and reduce unnecessary context.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this existing skill for trigger quality, progressive disclosure, resource organization, and unnecessary files. Suggest specific edits.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Publishing
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request before packaging and marketplace submission.
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate this skill folder for marketplace readiness. Check frontmatter, file organization, packaged contents, limitations, and security review notes.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for recurring product launch checklists.
output_examples.output: A concise skill plan with a SKILL.md outline, a launch workflow reference, and suggested checklist templates.

output_examples.input: Improve my existing database support skill.
output_examples.output: A review that identifies unclear triggers, oversized instructions, missing schema references, and cleanup tasks before packaging.
A validation summary covering required metadata, unnecessary files, package contents, and remaining manual review items.

best_practices: Start with real user examples before writing the skill instructions.
Keep SKILL.md concise and move detailed material into referenced files.
Inspect generated and packaged files before sharing a skill.
anti_patterns: Do not add broad documentation files that do not help the agent perform the task.
Do not hide important trigger conditions only inside the SKILL.md body.
Do not package folders that may contain secrets, private data, or unrelated files.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create and update AI skills with clear instructions, reusable resources, validation, and packaging steps.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it automatically write a finished skill?
faq.answer: No. It provides scaffolding and guidance, but the author must add domain-specific content and review the result.

faq.question: What files can it create?
faq.answer: The initializer can create a SKILL.md file and example scripts, references, and assets in a target directory.

faq.question: What should users check before packaging?
faq.answer: Users should remove unneeded files, inspect generated content, and confirm the folder contains no secrets or private data.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: It appears publishable with a filesystem warning because scripts can create files and package folder contents.
